notes: Recipe spawns 3 OpenCode `task` subagents in a single parent turn, each running bash sleep N for N=5/10/15 seconds. The OpenCode watcher polls the SQLite `session` table on a periodic cadence and reads `parent_id` per row; child rows surface as `EventNewSession` with `ParentSessionID = parent.id`, which the session detector promotes to `transcript_new` + `parent_linked` lifecycle events. The spec asserts: 3 transcript_new + 3 parent_linked (children visible to the SQLite watcher), parent stays working across the lifetimes of all three children (all_three_working invariant phase), child completions land in order (child1 < child2 < child3 by chained relative_to), parent terminal working→ready follows the last child. The 'no subagent rows linger after turn end' assertion from the spec is captured by parent_turn_end (state goes ready). The order in which children APPEAR in events.jsonl depends on the watcher's poll cadence (not necessarily completion order); the spec only chains COMPLETION order via same_session_as anchors on the longest sleeper finishing last. Delays are looser than claudecode because OpenCode's session-table poll runs less frequently than fswatcher inotify notifications.

text: User prompt drives parent ready → working
First subagent session row materializes with parent_id set
Second subagent session row materializes with parent_id set
Third subagent session row materializes with parent_id set

invariants: no state_transition to ready for parent
no state_transition to waiting for parent
text: Parent stays working across the lifetimes of all three children — the parent's outstanding `task` tool parts block until every child reports back
Shortest sleeper (~5s) finishes first: its bash sleep completes, the child's `task` tool returns, child working → ready (dashboard subagent count drops 3 → 2)
Mid sleeper (~10s) finishes next, in order after child1 (dashboard count 2 → 1)
After the LAST child reports back, the parent's `step-finish reason=stop` fires: working → ready. No subagent rows linger past this point — the dashboard shows the parent in ready with zero children.
